tests.name: baseline_build_loop_recorded_feature
tests.details: Completed full build_loop for uat-feat-1: tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit (stub), documentation_updated, feature_recorded; commit succeeded; build-progress.json features_completed = ['uat-feat-1']

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: When invoked as documented (`bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc` from project root), exit code is 1 due to a `cp ... files are identical (not copied)` error in the post-upgrade 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' step. Workaround: invoking from `$FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh` succeeds with RC=0 because SCRIPT_DIR resolves to a different path than the project's scripts/. The upgrade itself completes (state mutations, commit, validate) before this terminal failure. See bug entry.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh exits RC=1 when invoked from project's scripts/ dir (cp self-copy in helper-refresh step)
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Build a private_poc project via init.sh --non-interactive. 2) From $PROJECT_DIR run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc`. 3) Observe last log line: `cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and <PROJECT_DIR>/scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied).` and final RC=1.
framework_bugs.expected: Upgrade exits 0 on the documented invocation (`scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc`, per the script's own --help and the project's docs).
framework_bugs.actual: Upgrade exits 1. Root cause: scripts/upgrade-project.sh sets SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)". When the user runs the project's own copy of the script, SCRIPT_DIR resolves to <PROJECT>/scripts and the `cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/pending-approval.sh" "scripts/pending-approval.sh"` in the BL-009/BL-015 refresh block is a same-file copy, which fails on macOS BSD cp with 'files are identical'. The upgrade's mutations (phase-state.json, CLAUDE.md, commit) all complete before this point, so the project ends up logically upgraded but the script returns failure — any wrapper relying on RC will think the upgrade failed. Suggested fix: skip the cp when realpath src equals realpath dest, or always source helpers from a stable framework path (e.g., resolve via .claude/framework or orchestrator-source.json) rather than $SCRIPT_DIR. RC=0 reproduces only when the upgrade is invoked as `bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh ...`.

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/upgrade-project.sh --help recommends `scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc` (project-relative invocation), but that exact form trips the cp-self-copy bug above. The runbook's recommended `bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh ...` form does not — but no doc surfaces this gotcha to end users.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh --help / docs that document the upgrade command

documentation_gaps.gap: Upgrade post-step emits `[WARN] UAT platform unknown (intake-progress.json missing or lacks 'platform' field). Skipping reference copy` even on a freshly init.sh-created project where intake-progress.json is not produced by --non-interactive init. Either (a) --non-interactive should write intake-progress.json with platform, or (b) the warning should be silent / informational when intake-progress.json is absent on a non-interactive flow.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh UAT migration block / scripts/init.sh non-interactive intake-progress write

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets

No real credentials.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination

summary: Init/baseline/sentinel/state-preservation all PASS; one High bug surfaced — upgrade-project.sh exits RC=1 on the documented `scripts/upgrade-project.sh` invocation due to a same-file `cp` in the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh step (RC=0 reproduces only via `bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh`).
